$comment: Default settings for burnRat. User overrides are written to settings.json (gitignored). All values here are safe, tunable magic numbers — no logic depends on hardcoded numbers elsewhere.
$rateCacheWeightNote: How much cache tokens feed the burn signal that drives the rat: 0 = work only (input+output, lively small numbers), 1 = full cache included (~70x bigger numbers, more rat action). The thresholds in thresholds.json are calibrated for THIS weight — if you change it, retune them. Blend with any value in between.
$rateWindowSecondsNote: Smoothing time constant (tau) for the burn-rate EMA, in seconds — NOT a hard window. Larger = smoother/slower (takes ~tau seconds to forget a burst); smaller = twitchier. At 15 the EMA's peak magnitude matches the old 15s window, so the thresholds in thresholds.json stay calibrated.
$limitNote: The approaching-limit warnings auto-calibrate: instead of guessing a cap, the ceiling is your largest COMPLETED 5h block (total incl. cache) seen in the last `limitHistoryDays` of history, scanned once at startup. This adapts to your real usage (Anthropic doesn't publish caps). Trade-off: it reads oddly until you have history, and since your past peak is a lower bound on your true limit it can warn a bit early. `limitMinCredibleTokens` suppresses warnings until the learned ceiling looks real.
$planLimitsNote: Optional MANUAL override of the auto-calibrated ceiling, per plan (tokens/window, incl. cache). The active `plan`'s value, if > 0, is used as the cap instead of the learned ceiling; 0 = auto-adapt. Set this only if you actually know your cap.
$localServerNote: DEFAULT ON. A loopback-only (127.0.0.1) HTTP listener that Claude Code lifecycle hooks POST to, for instant, precise state edges (turn start/stop, tool use) instead of inferring everything from the JSONL tail. When enabled, burnRat installs its hook entries into ~/.claude/settings.json on launch (idempotent; backs the file up once) and binds a port (written to ~/.burnrat/runtime.json so the hook subcommand can find it). Turn it off from the tray ('Connect to Claude Code') — that uninstalls the hooks; disabled = no socket, no hooks (the old no-network behavior). `ports` is the candidate range; the first free one is bound.

localServer.$permissionTimeoutNote: How long (seconds) a tool-permission request waits for you to click Allow/Deny (or hit the hotkey) before the bridge returns 'no decision' and Claude Code falls back to its own terminal prompt. Keep below Claude's hook timeout (~10 min default).

$characterNote: Active character id — a folder under characters/ (dev: repo, prod: bundled resources, plus a user drop-in dir). The folder's character.json + ~10 PNGs supply every pose; swap live from the tray Character submenu.

display.$note: Rate-readout auto-scale. Prefer tok/sec (granular); drop to tok/min only when the per-second value would round below ~1. The gap between the two cutoffs is hysteresis so the unit doesn't flip-flop on a noisy signal.
